narrative: Ten closed trades deliver a 20% win rate at -6.19% average return (-3.77pp vs BTC) across a steady risk-off tape — BTC bleeding from $77K to $73K, Fear & Greed pinned in Extreme Fear all seven sessions. SHORTs outperformed LONGs (33% vs 14% WR, both small-n). The dominant loss shape is peak-phase LONG continuation or narrative-rotation entries lost mid-hold to within-sector inversion and narrative-carrier rotation, not to bad asset mechanics. The cleanest win was a direct-fire SHORT into ongoing structural bleed where no narrative tailwind defended the bid.
insights: `narrative_phase_aligned` shows -19.0pp edge (n_fired=7, 14.3% WR fired vs 33.3% WR missing). Narrative alignment is currently a loss-cluster, not an edge, because the aligned entries (ONDO, RENDER, TAO, NEAR) landed at PEAK or late-PEAK phase. V2 should treat 'narrative aligned AND phase=PEAK' as a yellow-flag combination that requires a fresh structural setup (ACCUMULATION or confirmed breakout) before firing — narrative-rotation continuation entries at peak get punished by sector inversion.

`pattern_tag_supports` (+28.6pp edge, n_fired=7) and `market_regime_aligned` (+25.0pp edge, n_fired=8) are the most reliable positive criteria with usable sample size. Both fired in the BCH and NEAR winners. The +88.9pp `both_tag` and +37.5pp `quant_regime_aligned` edges look stronger but n_fired ≤ 2 — promising signal, too thin to weight yet. V2 should treat the n≥7 criteria as near-mandatory entry filters and demote the n≤2 ones to tiebreakers until more data lands.

RIDE-then-loss rate is 65% (13 of 20 RIDE calls preceded a loss close). Stop discipline is fine — losses close at or near invalidation. The gap is mid-hold regime-degradation tracking: the engine rides through within-sector inversions (RENDER day 3, AI-compute sweep flipped to AI-compute drawdown) and narrative-carrier rotations (ONDO day 2, RWA flow rotated to tokenized stocks/ETFs) rather than closing on thesis break. V2 should run a daily 'is the regime that justified entry still intact?' check independent of price-vs-invalidation.

Direction skew is suggestive but n<30: SHORT WR 33% (n=3) beat LONG WR 14% (n=7). The lone SHORT WIN (BCH +13.17%) had three criteria firing into a clean structural bleed on risk-off tape, while both SHORT losses (GENIUS, the watchlist-promoted ZEC) were fades into known multi-contract name-farm clusters. Early signal: direct-fire SHORTs into structurally weak legacy names beat watchlist-decayed SHORTs into bsc/sol microcap clusters where retail rotates fresh contracts.

Name-farm cluster context was missing from the GENIUS SHORT thesis — the worst trade of the window at -24.11% return / MAE -33.86%. The active-topics tracker flagged GENIUS as a multi-day multi-contract recycling cluster days before fire (five distinct bsc contracts inside 72h). The CAPITULATION flow read was correct for the contract that flushed, but a fifth fresh contract reactivated the cluster within 24h and blew invalidation. V2 should cross-reference active-topics name-farm flags before any SHORT into bsc/sol microcaps — fading the listing pump on a recycling-cluster name has asymmetric blowup risk.

per_trade_postmortems.what_was_right: Top-trader L/S collapsed 1.43 points over 7 days into a -17.04% 7d drawdown, OI -9.88% confirming no leverage rebuild, funding -0.0336%/8h below the -0.0127% 7d average — smart-money exit and continuation positioning both clean at entry.
lessons: Legacy-alt names with no narrative tailwind on a sustained risk-off tape (BTC sub-$75K, breadth 3/20 green at fire) bleed cleanly to horizon — V2 can size up continuation SHORTs when narrative tailwind is explicitly absent rather than just unaligned.

Smart-money exit signalled by top L/S collapse of >1 point over 7 days alongside falling OI is a stronger continuation-SHORT signal than funding extremes alone — funding here was only modestly negative but the L/S move carried the thesis.

per_trade_postmortems.what_was_right: ACCUMULATION print resolved into +20.72% 24h rip on vol 4.53x with OI +41.79% 24h and short liqs $3.86M against p75 $1.38M — vol-and-OI-confirmed breakout, with funding +0.0085%/8h on day 2 showing fresh longs paying premium into the consolidation.
lessons: ACCUMULATION resolutions confirmed by vol-multiple AND OI build AND short liquidation cluster survive day-2 deep flushes (MAE -12.26% here) when OI unwinds without panic and funding stays positive — V2 should ride breakout-confirmed longs through MAE down to the structural invalidation rather than the first deep wick.

Late-PEAK narrative entries can still win on a 3d horizon when the asset structure carries the trade, but the 3d horizon matters — multi-week LONGs into PEAK-phase narratives carry sector-inversion risk that 3d doesn't.

per_trade_postmortems.what_was_right: CAPITULATION-shaped flow read at fire was structurally correct: -6.93% close with OI -6.43%, liquidations 2.76x p75 ($498K vs $180K), top L/S falling 0.37 points over 7d while OI had built 124% — smart-money exit into crowded retail leverage was the right shape.
per_trade_postmortems.what_went_wrong: Asset thesis missed that GENIUS was a known multi-contract recycling cluster (five distinct bsc contracts inside 72h, flagged in active-topics days prior) — a fifth fresh contract reactivated the cluster on day 2 with vol_ratio 2.38x clearing the breakout gate, blowing through the $0.68 invalidation by 7% and printing MAE -33.86%.
lessons: Before SHORTing a recently-listed bsc/sol microcap, check active-topics for an existing name-farm flag — fading the listing pump on a recycling-cluster name is asymmetric: the structural read can be correct for the contract that flushed and still get blown by a fresh contract reactivating the same ticker.

Watchlist-promoted SHORTs into thin-float clusters should carry a wider invalidation than a recent intraday bounce high — the $0.68 invalidation was 17% above fire, not enough room for cluster-reactivation volatility on a sub-$1 microcap with 124% OI build.

per_trade_postmortems.what_was_right: Named risks captured the RWA PEAK saturation risk and the rolled-OUT-of-ACCUMULATION structural concern at fire — the risk register knew this was a late-cycle narrative-rotation trade without a fresh structural setup.
per_trade_postmortems.what_went_wrong: Asset structure was thin (rolled out of yesterday's ACCUMULATION, top L/S 1.79 with -0.20 7d drift, only a shallow one-day re-entry to trending) and the RWA narrative carrier rotated mid-hold to tokenized stocks ($1.2B) and RWA ETFs ($425M) rather than token-direct names — invalidation $0.41 breached by 7% on day 2, MFE +1.51% never extending.
lessons: Narrative-rotation LONG entries at PEAK phase with no fresh structural setup (ACCUMULATION or breakout) and named-and-acknowledged late-cycle risk should fire smaller-than-default or pass — the named risks register here predicted the failure mode, the trade fired anyway.

RWA-class narrative tailwinds can rotate from token-direct to wrapped/ETF carriers within days; V2 should track which carrier the inflows are landing in (token-direct vs derivative wrapper) and downgrade conviction on token-direct LONGs when the carrier shifts.

per_trade_postmortems.what_was_right: At fire, AI-compute peer set was leading the tape — RENDER +14.2% 24h on $258M vol with vol/mcap 0.21, sector sweeping 7 of 8 winner slots on a 1/20 green day, Decentralized compute at PEAK second session with token burns +278% YoY. The cross-sector rotation read was structurally clean.
per_trade_postmortems.what_went_wrong: Sector regime inverted mid-hold: day 1 wick to $1.88 left MAE -17.9% but daily close held above invalidation; day 3 AI-compute distributed and RENDER led the sector lower at -12.5%, the biggest loser among the same names that had swept the winners. Closed on thesis break before stop printed.
lessons: When entry thesis rests on a same-day sector sweep (7 of 8 winners), the regime context is a single tape session — V2 should require sector-leadership confirmation across two sessions before firing a narrative-rotation LONG, or shorten horizon to 3d to limit sector-inversion exposure.

PEAK-phase narrative entries on a 1/20-green tape (broad risk-off with sector idiosyncratic) should fire smaller — when the rest of the universe is bleeding, the sector sweep is fragile and inverts on any sector-internal supply event.

regime_observations: BTC drew down from $77,417 (May 22) to $73,314 (May 29), about -5% over the audit window — chop within a sustained bleed, not collapse. Three sub-$75K closes confirmed the lower regime.
Fear & Greed pinned at Extreme Fear (22-25) across all seven sessions. F&G never validated a risk-on regime change, so any LONG entry was fighting tape-wide sentiment.

AI / Decentralized compute sector phase transitioned Rising → Peak → Fading inside the window. NEAR caught the Rising/early-Peak leg and won on 3d horizon; RENDER caught the Peak → Fading inversion at fire and lost on 7d.

RWA narrative-carrier rotated from token-direct to tokenized stocks ($1.2B inflows) and RWA ETFs ($425M inflows) during the window. The ONDO LONG was firing into the legacy carrier just as the flow rotated to wrappers.

BTC ETF outflow streak extended to nine sessions with -$223M on May 29 ($2.8B cumulative). Institutional risk-off confirmed throughout — no session in the window saw inflow that would have supported broad-alt continuation LONGs.
